channel.title: US Top News and Analysis
channel.description: CNBC is the world leader in business news and real-time financial market coverage. Find fast, actionable information.

item.title: U.S., Iran agree on roadmap for final deal and plan to end military operations in Lebanon
item.description: The talks were the first round of negotiations under a memorandum of understanding signed last week, which extended a tenuous ceasefire by 60 days.

item.title: Stock futures fall as investors weigh Iran deal progress and await key reading on inflation: Live updates
item.description: U.S. equity futures were lower on Monday as Wall Street awaited the release of inflation data that is closely watched by the Federal Reserve.

item.title: Oil falls as Qatar, Pakistan announce 60-day roadmap for U.S.-Iran deal
item.description: Oil prices fluctuated on Monday after U.S. President Donald Trump threatened renewed military action against Iran.

item.title: UK Prime Minister Keir Starmer set to outline exit plan as pressure to quit builds
item.description: U.K. Prime Minister Keir Starmer is expected to set out a timetable for his departure later.

item.title: CNBC Daily Open: U.K.'s Keir Starmer on the verge of quitting
item.description: A war of words in Westminster could cost U.K. Prime Minister Keir Starmer his job, as the leader looks set to resign amid pressure from within his party.

item.title: Five top tech stocks with major upside, Bank of America says
item.description: Bank of America reveals its top tech stocks heading into the second half.

item.title: Colombian right-wing candidate De La Espriella wins tight presidential race
item.description: Colombian right-wing candidate Abelardo De La Espriella, who is backed by Donald Trump, appears to have clinched a narrow victory in the presidential election.

item.title: China imposes trade curbs on dozens of U.S. firms in retaliation for Pentagon blacklist
item.description: The Pentagon earlier this month updated its so-called 1260H list by adding a slew of Chinese technology companies to a roster of entities it believes have aided Beijing's military.

item.title: Why a 66-year-old water treaty is becoming the latest India-Pakistan flashpoint
item.description: India and Pakistan are locked in a dispute over the Indus Water Treaty after New Delhi suspended the pact, raising concerns about regional stability.

item.title: Goldman Sachs is betting that a recent merger will drive sharp gains for this travel stock
item.description: The bank this week upgraded the airline to buy from neutral.

item.title: The best parents and leaders use a 3-step formula to help people handle uncertainty, says Dr. Becky Kennedy
item.description: Even if you can't control an outcome, you can still help your child or co-worker feel more at ease with uncertainty, just by how you describe a situation.

item.title: 'Fun dads' get 5 things right about parenting that many people forget—'they're worth borrowing' this Father's Day, says expert
item.description: Many parents spend their days managing schedules, chores and responsibilities. This Father's Day, a parenting expert shares five "fun dad" habits that can increase joy and strengthen parent-child relationships.

item.title: 'Regime change but in a velvet glove': How Kevin Warsh has set out to remake the Fed
item.description: The first big announced changes point toward a quiet revolution, with task forces set up to rethink virtually everything done at the Fed.

item.title: Here are 3 big things to watch in the stock market this coming week
item.description: FedEx reports earnings, and the Fed's preferred inflation gauge is due out.

item.title: Pixar's 'Toy Story 5' lassos biggest opening weekend in franchise history with $160 million haul
item.description: Disney and Pixar's "Toy Story 5" tallied $160 million domestically during its opening weekend, the highest debut in franchise history.

item.title: No one wants AI data centers on Earth. Do they make sense in space?
item.description: Elon Musk's SpaceX is betting big on orbital AI data centers. The public doesn't want them on Earth, but the economic case for space-based is questionable.

item.title: 54 injured and 18 missing after explosion at Qatar LNG site, authorities say
item.description: 54 people have been injured while 18 are missing after an explosion at Qatar's core LNG processing site of Ras Laffan on Sunday, authorities said.

item.title: Raising the minimum wage has been a big political winner. Now it's running into resistance
item.description: Raising the minimum wage has been a progressive policy winner at the ballot box, but recent losses suggest an economic mood slowing its political momentum.

item.title: Next week's inflation data just got even more important. Here's what's ahead for the market
item.description: Investors will now laser in on any signs that the central bank could hike interest rates against a backdrop of a buoyant stock market.

item.title: UK's Starmer considers political future, could decide as soon as Monday
item.description: The threat to Starmer's position increased sharply on Friday when former Greater Manchester Mayor Andy Burnham won a seat in parliament.

item.title: The AI trade has left the hyperscalers in the dust. What will it take for that to change?
item.description: In his Sunday column for Investing Club subscribers, Jim Cramer examines the market's love affair with memory and semi-cap equipment stocks.

item.title: You can expect gas prices to remain elevated. Here's how to save
item.description: Maximize savings at the pump with these retail memberships, wholesalers and rewards credit cards.

item.title: Top Wall Street analysts like these 3 dividend stocks for solid returns
item.description: TipRanks discusses three dividend-paying stocks.

item.title: Serena Williams to play singles at Wimbledon via wild card
item.description: Williams, 44, will compete with her sister Venus in doubles, as the pair also accepted a wild-card entry into that draw.

item.title: AI buildout gives tech investors new reasons to watch bond market
item.description: Tech giants are depleting cash reserves and raising debt in their ambitious data center buildouts, a dynamic that's forcing investors to watch interest rates.

item.title: Republicans buy crypto more than Democrats, data shows — what's driving the divide
item.description: "There's a massive, massive partisan gap," of crypto adoption between Democrats and Republicans. Here's why.

item.title: The budget airline model in the U.S. is running out of runway
item.description: Spirit's bankruptcy as United and Delta fly high suggests there's more to success in airline industry than low fares, and failure isn't about jet fuel spikes.

item.title: SNAP restrictions could change what shoppers buy — and food giants are watching
item.description: SNAP food restrictions are spreading to more states, pressuring major food and beverage as consumers shift spending away from soda, candy and processed foods.

item.title: She saved $24,000 to launch a craft festival in an old Joann store: 'This is my dream come true'
item.description: "This is my dream come true," says Tetef, a long-time events producer. "I just want to go to a thing where everyone's sitting down making stuff."

item.title: Bitcoin's future as revolutionary as the smartphone, according to CoinDesk
item.description: CoinDesk’s president of indices and data has a message for investors: Don’t count out bitcoin.
